memoryArtifacts.headline: Image of CP 25/1/288/47, number 633.
memoryArtifacts.description:
CP 25/1/288/47, number 633.
Link:	Image of document at AALT
County:	Cambridgeshire. Essex.
Place:	Westminster.
Date:	Two weeks from Easter, 38 Edward III [7 April 1364].
Parties:	William de Quenton', knight, and Isabel, his wife, querents, and John de Olneye, Nicholas de Cogenho and John Cook' of Comberton', deforciants.
Property:	The manor of Comberton' in the county of Cambridge and the manor of Whitrothyng and the advowson of the church of the same manor in the county of Essex.
Action:	Plea of covenant.
Agreement:	William has acknowledged the manors and advowson to be the right of John, Nicholas and John, as those which John, Nicholas and John have of his gift.
For this:	John, Nicholas and John have granted to William and Isabel the manors and advowson and have rendered them to them in the court, to hold to William and Isabel, of the lord king and his heirs for the lives of William and Isabel. And after the decease of William and Isabel the manors and advowson shall remain to Henry, son of Henry Grene of Isham, and the heirs of his body, to hold of the lord king and his heirs for ever. In default of such heirs, remainder to Henry Grene the elder, the father of the aforesaid Henry, son of Henry, and his heirs.
Note:	This agreement was made by the command of the lord king.

Standardised forms of names. (These are tentative suggestions, intended only as a finding aid.)
Persons:	William de Quinton, Isabel de Quinton, John de Olney, Nicholas de Cogenhoe, John Cook, Henry Green
Places:	Comberton, White Roding, Isham (in Northamptonshire)

zh.Isham, Northamptonshire, England: 英國諾薩普頓史爾易沙安
zh.lifeSummary: Henry del Grene of Isham出生在英國。他與Mrs. Henry del Grene結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1352年在英國諾薩普頓史爾易沙安。

zh-hans.Isham, Northamptonshire, England: 英国諾薩普頓史爾易沙安
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Henry del Grene of Isham出生在英国。他与Mrs. Henry del Grene结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1352年在英国諾薩普頓史爾易沙安。

nameMeanings.name: del
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French (Lorraine): variant of the habitational name Delle 2 or a masculine form of a homonymous topographic name. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Spanish or Italian preposition with fused definite article del ‘of the’, which forms first part of surnames such as Del Cid and Del Signore , has apparently been in some instances counted as an independent surname.

variants.name: Delle
meanings: German: habitational name from a place so named in North Rhine-Westphalia or a topographic name (see   Dell  ).

French (Lorraine): habitational name from Delle, a place in Territoire de Belfort. Alternatively, a topographic name, with fused preposition d(e) ‘from’, for someone who lived near a prominent alder tree or trees, from elle, a derivative of Middle High German erle ‘alder’.

nameMeanings.name: Grene
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Green
meanings: English: either a nickname for someone who was fond of dressing in this color (Old English grēne) or was young or immature, or who had played the part of the ‘Green Man’ in the May Day celebrations, or a topographic name for someone who lived near a village green (Middle English grene, a transferred use of the color term). This is one of the most common and widespread of English surnames. In North America it has assimilated cognates from other languages, notably German Grün (see Gruen ) and Dutch Groen ; compare 7 below. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
English: alternatively, from a Middle English personal name Grene.
Irish: adopted for Ó hUainín ‘descendant of Uainín’, a personal name from a pet form of uaine ‘green’, see Honan .

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Henry del Grene of Isham was born in England. He married Mrs. Henry del Grene. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died about 1352, in Isham, Northamptonshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Henry del Grene of Isham est né à Angleterre. Il a épousé Mrs. Henry del Grene. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé environ 1352, à Isham, Northamptonshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Henry del Grene of Isham wurde in England geboren. Er hat Mrs. Henry del Grene geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb um 1352 in Isham, Northamptonshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Henry del Grene of Isham è nato il nel England. Lui sposato Mrs. Henry del Grene. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì circa 1352, nel Isham, Northamptonshire, England.

ja.Isham, Northamptonshire, England: イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアイシャム
ja.lifeSummary: Henry del Grene of Ishamはイギリスで生まれた。Mrs. Henry del Greneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は約1352年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアイシャムで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Henry del Grene of Isham(은)는 영국에서 태어났다. 그는 Mrs. Henry del Grene(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1352년 영국 노르섬프토실르 이샤에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Henry del Grene of Isham nasceu em Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Mrs. Henry del Grene. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1352, em Isham, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Henry del Grene of Isham родился в местности Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Henry del Grene. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер около 1352 г. в местности ишам, нортамптоншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Henry del Grene of Isham nació en Inglaterra. Se casó con Mrs. Henry del Grene. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió aproximadamente 1352, en Isham, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.
